title: A Night at the Traditional Market

all.en: On Friday evening, my cousin from Canada wanted to experience a traditional Taiwanese night market.
all.zh: 星期五傍晚，我從加拿大來的表弟想體驗傳統的台灣夜市。

all.en: We arrived just after sunset, when the lanterns were beginning to light up the narrow streets.
all.zh: 我們在日落後不久抵達，這時燈籠正開始照亮狹窄的街道。

all.en: The smell of grilled squid, stinky tofu, and fried chicken filled the warm evening air.
all.zh: 烤魷魚、臭豆腐與炸雞的香氣瀰漫在溫暖的夜晚空氣中。

all.en: My cousin was nervous about trying stinky tofu, but he bravely took one bite.
all.zh: 表弟對嘗試臭豆腐感到緊張，但他勇敢地咬了一口。

all.en: To his surprise, he said the flavor was much milder than the strong smell suggested.
all.zh: 令他驚訝的是，他說味道比那強烈的氣味溫和得多。

all.en: We waited fifteen minutes in line for a famous bubble tea shop with chewy tapioca balls.
all.zh: 我們在一家以Q彈珍珠聞名的珍珠奶茶店排了十五分鐘的隊。

all.en: A vendor demonstrated how to make oyster omelets on a huge, sizzling iron pan.
all.zh: 一位攤販示範如何在巨大、嘶嘶作響的鐵板上製作蚵仔煎。

all.en: We also played a small game where you try to catch goldfish with a paper net.
all.zh: 我們還玩了一個小遊戲，要用紙網撈金魚。

all.en: My cousin won a stuffed bear at a balloon-popping booth and felt very proud.
all.zh: 表弟在射氣球攤位贏得了一隻填充小熊，感到非常驕傲。

all.en: By the end of the night, our hands were full of snacks and small souvenirs.
all.zh: 到了夜晚結束時，我們手上滿是小吃與小紀念品。

all.en: He told me that the night market was the most exciting part of his entire trip.
all.zh: 他告訴我，夜市是他整趟旅程中最令人興奮的部分。
